subject: Chemistry
title: Brownian Motion: Proof Atoms Exist
description: Learn Brownian Motion: Proof Atoms Exist in one minute.

hook.text: In 1827, Robert Brown watched pollen grains jiggle in water. He couldn't explain why—but Einstein could.

buildup.text: Brown saw tiny pollen particles moving randomly in water under a microscope. No force seemed to push them.

discovery.text: In 1905, Einstein proved the jiggling was caused by invisible water molecules bombarding the pollen from all sides.

twist.text: Many scientists still doubted atoms existed in 1905. Einstein's math showed the jiggle matched atomic theory perfectly.

climax.text: Jean Perrin confirmed Einstein's predictions experimentally in 1908—and won the Nobel Prize for proving atoms are real.

punchline.text: Pollen jiggles, math explains why. Invisible molecules pushing visible grains.

quiz.question: How did Einstein explain Brownian motion?
options: Invisible water molecules randomly bombard visible particles from all sides
Tiny electrical charges in water repel the pollen
Heat convection currents in the water push particles around
